Oregon Joint Community Benefits and Impacts Group for Tribal Nations Meeting

June 26, 2026 - 9:00 a.m. to 11:00 a.m. PT

Online via Zoom

Meeting ID: 83532601961

Passcode: 887899

Tribal relations

As part of our focus on equity, we aim to build an inclusive and accessible process with Tribes, Tribal governments, agencies and community organizations for consultation and collaboration. 

These efforts are taking shape through cooperation and meaningful engagement opportunities in our energy planning processes.

See how Yakama Nation is saving on energy costs with improved lighting

Choose your state to learn more

    1. Oregon
    2. California

    Oregon Tribal Nations Community Benefits and Impacts & Clean Energy Planning

    We seek expanded engagement with Oregon tribes for a clean energy future that focuses on equity to build an inclusive and accessible process for consultation and collaboration.

    Throughout the development of the 2023 CEP, we will continue to seek and incorporate feedback, which will help develop our specific actions, targets and other aspects of our clean energy plan.

    This includes: 

    • Increasing participation from communities that have not traditionally participated in utility planning processes; 
    • Providing us with a better understanding of community needs and perspectives; 
    • Identifying barriers to participation and input on how to address these barriers; 
    • Acting as a conduit to exchange information and ideas between us and partner communities; and 
    • Assisting with community outreach. 

    To join the distribution list to learn more about these essential efforts, or for questions regarding the information provided below, please email us.

    Biennial report

    2024 Community Benefits Biennial Report

    Oregon Tribal Nations Community Benefits and Impacts Advisory Group - meetings and workshops

    The following provides a proposed meeting schedule, subject to change based on participants' input and feedback: 

    1/30/2026

    9:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m.; January meeting (slides and public notes)

    2/27/2026

    CANCELLED: February meeting

    3/27/2026

    CANCELLED: March meeting

    4/25/2026

    9:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m.; April meeting (slides and notes)

    5/20/2026

    1:00 p.m. - 4:00 p.m.; Joint Community Benefits and Impacts Group Meeting (slides)

    6/26/2026

    9:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m.; June meeting

    7/31/2026

    9:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m.; July meeting

    9:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m.; August meeting

    9/18/2026

    		 9:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m.; September meeting

    10/30/2026 9:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m.; October meeting
    12/18/2026

    9:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m.; December meeting

    Tribal Land Reports

    Pacific Power is regulated by the California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC). For more information on the CPUC’s Tribal consultation, resources and policy, go to CPUC’s Tribal website.

    Transfer land notifications

    None

    Reports

    Pacific Power reports are pursuant to CPUC Resolution E-5076 and the CPUC’s Tribal Land Transfer Policy.

    2026

    • First Quarter

    2025

    • First Quarter | Second Quarter | Third Quarter | Fourth Quarter

    2024

    • First Quarter | Second Quarter | Third Quarter | Fourth Quarter

    2023

    • First Quarter | Second Quarter | Third Quarter | Fourth Quarter

    2022

    • First Quarter | Second Quarter | Third Quarter | Fourth Quarter

Pacific Power contacts

Regional Business Managers live within the communities we serve. Check out the contact list and map for your local Regional Business Manager.
